content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach reaches for AoA-style moves early — Turn 4 is a clean find-the-want-behind-the-should move, and Turn 5 yields concrete texture (the lake, sleeping deeply). But the coach fails to stay with the want; instead of letting it be felt, Turn 6 issues a verdict ('that's not selfish'), engaging the question on its terms and trapping the coach in reassurance mode. Turn 8 then pivots to fear without ever letting the want land in the body. Turn 10's 'trust your partner can be with that' is premature — it leaps to partner-empathy before the want is felt. Turn 12 compounds this by jumping straight to the spiritual/generous reframe ('deep generosity in you', 'so much more to give') exactly the trap noted in the scenario. So the moves are AoA-shaped but the sequence collapses: want is touched, then abandoned for reassurance, fear, partner-framing, and spiritual reframe — skipping the step of letting the surface want be fully felt.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 5, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a definition question rather than refusing the selfish-frame; soft but doesn't redirect to want."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean find-the-want move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Issues a verdict on the selfish question and reassures; abandons the want just as it surfaced."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Pivots to fear; reasonable move but skips letting the want itself be felt."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Jumps to partner-empathy ('trust that your partner can be with that') before the want is felt — trips dont-jump-to-compassion."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Spiritual-narcissism trap: reframes the want as generosity and promises return-on-investment ('so much more to give') instead of trusting the want itself."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "childhood-imprinting", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "notes": "Hit rate roughly 50%: one strong move (T4), one neutral (T8), three misses (T6, T10, T12). Coach reaches for the right kind of moves but doesn't earn them by staying with the want."
}
